metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5121
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "Double-labeling of sensory neurons with antibodies against CRMP-2 and the synaptic vesicle marker synaptophysin (Ichikawa et al., 1991) revealed extensive colocalization of CRMP-2 in synaptophysin-positive puncta (Fig. 6B), wherein 85±12% (n=468) of synapses were labeled with both proteins (Fig. 6D). To test whether endogenous CRMP-2 was targeted to functionally active synapses and/or release sites, neurons were loaded with the membrane-bound dye FM4-64 by stimulation with 90 mM KCl. Neurons loaded with FM dye were then fixed and labeled with an antibody against CRMP-2 (Fig. 6Ci). Of the functionally active FM-dye-loaded release sites, 78±17% (n=347) were positive for CRMP-2, thus indicating that CRMP-2 is targeted to functional synapses (Fig. 6Ciii,D).". Experimental description: M&M: "C57BL/6 mice (3-6 months old) and Sprague-Dawley rats (100-150 g) were purchased from Harlan Laboratories (Indianapolis, IN)."

antibody:
polyclonal CRMP-2 (Cell Signaling Technology, MA)
monoclonal CRMP-2 (Abcam, Cambridge, MA)

Validated siRNAs against the rat or mouse CRMP-2 [5'-ACTCCTTCCTCGTGTACAT-3'] sequence (Nishimura et al., 2003) were from Invitrogen.

BLAST confirmed ACTCCTTCCTCGTGTACAT matched GenBank accession NM_001378767 --> Entrez Gene 12934 (Dpysl2)
